headline: Public Health Budget Transparency Ranks 42nd of 54 Countries Assessed
context: Malaysia scored poorly on global health budget transparency index with limited public access to expenditure data.

cards.text: Malaysia ranked 42nd of 54 countries on health budget transparency index in 2025
cards.sub: Citizens cannot access detailed breakdowns of how RM38 billion health budget is spent

cards.h: MOH publishes only aggregate budget figures not hospital-level or program-level expenditure data
cards.s: Lack of granularity prevents meaningful public accountability for health spending
cards.d: MOH publishes only aggregate budget figures not hospital-level or program-level expenditure data Lack of granularity prevents meaningful public accountability for health spending

cards.h: 3 Auditor General reports since 2020 identified RM2.8 billion in questionable health expenditures
cards.s: Opacity enables misallocation and waste that transparent systems would prevent
cards.d: 3 Auditor General reports since 2020 identified RM2.8 billion in questionable health expenditures Opacity enables misallocation and waste that transparent systems would prevent

cards.h: Mexico publishes real-time health expenditure dashboards accessible to all citizens since 2019
cards.s: Open data approach enabled citizen monitoring that reduced procurement irregularities 28%
cards.d: Mexico publishes real-time health expenditure dashboards accessible to all citizens since 2019 Open data approach enabled citizen monitoring that reduced procurement irregularities 28%

cards.text: A government that hides how it spends health money is a government that does not trust its own citizens
Should MOH publish detailed real-time health expenditure data accessible to all Malaysians
